◇ Glaze Chemist
Home People Books Concepts Lineage

People

61 figures in glaze chemistry
Abu'l-Qasim
author, ceramist
Persian - Medieval
Kashan tile workshops
Wrote the first known treatise on ceramic technology in 1301, documenting the glaze and tile-making traditions of Kashan, Persia.
medievallusterhistoricalfoundationalpersian
Adelaide Alsop Robineau
studio potter, editor, researcher
American - 19th Century
University City / Keramic Studio magazine
Editor of Keramic Studio (precursor to Ceramics Monthly).
crystallineporcelainhistoricalfoundational
Bernard Leach
studio potter, author, educator
British - 20th Century
Leach Pottery, St Ives
Usually discussed aesthetically but contains serious chemistry; proto-terroir glaze work before the concept existed.
ash-glazesstudiohistoricalbeloved
Byron Temple
studio potter, researcher
American - 20th Century
Alfred University
Did serious practical research on salt and soda glaze chemistry at high temperatures.
salt-glazeatmosphericstudio
C.W. Parmelee
author, ceramic scientist
American - 20th Century
University of Illinois
Long considered the most comprehensive technical reference on glaze science from an industrial standpoint.
technicalindustrialcultfoundational
Charles Fergus Binns
educator, author, ceramic scientist
British-American - 19th Century
Alfred University / New York State College of Ceramics
Son of the director of Royal Worcester.
educationhistoricalfoundationalalfred-university
Clary Illian
studio potter, author, educator
American - Contemporary
Studied under Warren MacKenzie, who studied under Bernard Leach.
materialscontemporarystudioleach-tradition
Coll Minogue
author, studio potter
Irish - Contemporary
Co-authored key wood-fire chemistry texts with Robert Sanderson; practical and analytical approach to ash and atmospheric glazes.
wood-fireash-glazesatmospheric
Daniel Rhodes
author, educator
American - 20th Century
Alfred University
Standard text for generations; described as having a voice — unhurried, curious, authoritative.
studiofoundationalbeloved
Dave Finkelnburg
educator, researcher
American - Contemporary
Active educator who writes extensively about glaze fit, thermal expansion, crazing vs.
analyticalglaze-fiteducationcontemporary
David Green
author, educator
British - 20th Century
Wrote one of the clearest explanations of glaze chemistry for studio potters ever published.
educationstudiobritish
Derek Au (Philipau)
developer, educator
American - Contemporary
Glazy.org
Created and maintains Glazy.org, the world's largest open ceramics database with 340K+ entries.
databasecontemporaryopen-sourcefoundational
Edward Orton Jr.
ceramic scientist, researcher
American - 19th Century
Ohio State University / Orton Ceramic Foundation
Cone system so embedded in practice that people forget it was a significant scientific contribution.
measurementhistoricalfoundational
Emmanuel Cooper
author, educator, editor
British - 20th Century
Ceramic Review / City of Westminster College
Bridged chemistry, history, and practice across multiple books.
studiohistorybeloved
F.H. Norton
author, ceramic engineer
American - 20th Century
MIT
Rigorous engineering texts that influenced generations of ceramic scientists and indirectly shaped studio practice through Alfred and Ohio State pr...
technicalindustrialacademic
Felix Singer
ceramic engineer, author
German-British - 20th Century
Co-authored with his son W.L.
engineeringfoundationalhistoricalacademic
Frank Hamer
author
British - 20th Century
Often underappreciated relative to actual influence.
referencefoundationalbeloved
Gabriel Kline
author, studio potter, educator
American - Contemporary
Wrote the modern bible of crystalline glaze chemistry.
crystallinespecialtycontemporary
Gail Nichols
studio potter, author, educator
Australian - Contemporary
Wrote the definitive book on soda glazing chemistry.
soda-firingatmosphericcontemporaryaustralian
Greg Daly
studio potter, author, educator
Australian - Contemporary
Leading authority on luster glazes and their chemistry.
lusterspecialtycontemporaryaustralian
Hein Severijns
developer
Dutch - Contemporary
Created GlazeMaster, one of the earliest glaze calculation software packages alongside Hansen's Insight.
softwarecomputationalcontemporary
Henrik Norsker
author, researcher, development worker
Danish - Contemporary
Co-authored Glazes for the Self-Reliant Potter (with James Danisch) — how to develop glazes from local rocks, ash, and clay when commercial materia...
local-materialsgeologycontemporarydevelopment
Herbert Sanders
author
American - 20th Century
San Jose State University
Bridged Eastern glaze traditions with Western scientific analysis at a time when that crossover was rare.
specialtyhistoricaljapanese
Hermann August Seger
chemist, researcher
German - 19th Century
Royal Porcelain Factory, Berlin (KPM)
Established the foundational UMF system that underpins rational glaze calculation; contribution considered irreplaceable and enduring for 150+ years
umfhistoricalfoundational
Ian Currie
author, researcher
Australian - Contemporary
Developed a genuinely elegant contribution to empirical glaze research.
systematictestingcontemporarybeloved
Jack Troy
author, studio potter
American - Contemporary
Juniata College
Documented wood-fire and salt-fire chemistry with more analytical rigor than most atmospheric firing literature.
wood-fireatmosphericstudio
James Chappell
author
American - 20th Century
Wrote the single most comprehensive recipe reference book in studio ceramics — hundreds of recipes with chemistry, organized by type, cone, and atm...
recipesstudioreference
Janet Hamer
author
British - 20th Century
Co-authored The Potter's Dictionary with Frank Hamer; the collaboration produced one of the most enduring reference works in ceramics.
referencefoundational
Jeff Zamek
author
American - Contemporary
More recent practical reference covering materials and glaze formulation together.
practicalstudiocontemporary
John Britt
author, educator
American - Contemporary
Most-used studio references of last two decades.
studiocontemporarypopularbeloved
John Hesselberth
author
American - Contemporary
Co-authored the cone 6 reference that validated middle-temperature functional ware as worthy of rigorous chemistry attention.
cone-6contemporary
John Neely
educator, researcher
American - Contemporary
Utah State University
Brought spectroscopic methods to questions that previously relied on visual assessment.
wood-fireatmosphericanalyticalcontemporary
Josiah Wedgwood
industrialist, researcher, inventor
British - 18th Century
Wedgwood / Etruria Works
Invented systematic experimental method for ceramics a century before anyone else.
experimentalindustrialhistoricalfoundational
Linda Bloomfield
author, educator
British - Contemporary
Particularly strong on colorant chemistry; clean modern treatment bridging scientific explanation and studio practice.
colorantscontemporarystudio
Maija Grotell
educator, studio potter
Finnish-American - 20th Century
Cranbrook Academy of Art
Known as the 'Mother of American Ceramics'; revolutionary educator focused on glaze chemistry.
historicaleducatorfoundational
Masakazu Kusakabe
studio potter, author, educator
Japanese - Contemporary
Co-authored Japanese Wood-Fired Ceramics (with Marc Lancet) — scientific treatment of anagama and noborigama atmosphere chemistry, ash deposit mech...
wood-firekiln-sciencecontemporaryjapanese
Michael Cardew
studio potter, author
British - 20th Century
Wenford Bridge Pottery
Known as a 'glaze wizard' in studio pottery.
studiohistorical
Mimi Obstler
author, educator
American - Contemporary
Bridges materials science and studio practice in a way that's genuinely accessible.
material-scienceeducationcontemporarystudio
Nigel Wood
scholar, author, researcher
British - Contemporary
University of Westminster
Most rigorous scientific treatment of classical Chinese ceramic chemistry in English.
historical-reconstructionscholarlychinesebelovedcult
Paul Soldner
studio potter, educator, researcher
American - 20th Century
Scripps College
Transformed Western raku practice and forced rethinking of low-fire reduction chemistry.
rakureductionstudiofoundational
Pete Pinnell
educator, researcher, studio potter
American - Contemporary
University of Nebraska-Lincoln
One of the most respected living glaze chemistry educators.
educationcrystallinesystematiccontemporary
Phil Rogers
studio potter, author
British (Welsh) - 20th Century
Different from Tichane's historical reconstruction approach — Phil's Ash Glazes is practical wood-ash chemistry focused on studio use.
ashwood-firestudiobritish
Pieter Mostert
researcher, developer
South African - Contemporary
Active researcher doing computational analysis of the Glazy dataset.
computationaldata-sciencecontemporaryopen-source
Père d'Entrecolles (François Xavier d'Entrecolles)
observer, author
French - 17th Century
Jesuit Mission, Jingdezhen
Jesuit missionary stationed in Jingdezhen, China's porcelain capital.
porcelainhistoricalfoundationalchinese
R.T. Stull
ceramic engineer, researcher
American - Early 20th Century
University of Illinois
Published two landmark papers in the Transactions of the American Ceramic Society: Vol.
calculationfoundationalhistoricalacademicopen-access
Richard Zakin
author, educator
American - 20th Century
SUNY Oswego
Addressed oxidation firing chemistry seriously at a time when most literature assumed reduction.
oxidationelectric-kilnstudio
Robert Desjardins
software developer, database developer
Canadian - Contemporary
Helped build the computational infrastructure underlying Digitalfire.
digitalsoftwarecontemporary
Robert Sanderson
author
British - Contemporary
Co-authored wood-fire chemistry texts with Coll Minogue; systematic documentation of ash glaze behavior.
wood-fireash-glazesatmospheric
Robert Tichane
author, researcher
American - 20th Century
Self-published, small print runs, out of print.
historical-reconstructionashreductioncultbeloved
Robin Hopper
author, educator, studio potter
Canadian - 20th Century
One of the most systematic treatments of color in glazes, organized around the periodic table of colorants.
colorantsstudiobeloved
Ron Roy
researcher, educator
Canadian - 20th Century
Co-developer of the cone 6 oxidation movement.
cone-6testingsafetycontemporary
Shoji Hamada
studio potter
Japanese - 20th Century
Mashiko, Japan
Left almost no written technical documentation by design, but posthumous analytical work on his glazes is chemically revealing — particularly his u...
ash-glazesjapanesestudiohistorical
Steve Branfman
author, studio potter, educator
American - Contemporary
The Potters Shop, Needham MA
Wrote the definitive practical guide to raku chemistry — carbon trapping, metallic lusters, copper reduction, post-firing chemistry.
rakulow-firereductioncontemporary
Taxile Doat
studio potter, author, researcher
French - 19th Century
Sèvres Porcelain Manufactory / University City
Master ceramist at Sèvres who figured out zinc crystal growth before anyone had the phase diagrams to explain why.
crystallinehistoricalfoundationalfrench
Tony Birks
author
British - 20th Century
Reached a mass audience and introduced glaze chemistry to potters who might not have sought it out.
popularintroductory
Tony Hansen
author, software developer, educator
Canadian - Contemporary
Digitalfire
Arguably done more than anyone in the modern era to make glaze chemistry computationally accessible.
digitalsoftwarecontemporaryfoundational
Val Cushing
educator, author
American - 20th Century
Alfred University
Handbook circulated through Alfred's orbit, not commercially published.
studiobelovedalfred-universitycult
W. David Kingery
author, ceramic scientist, researcher
American - 20th Century
MIT / University of Arizona
THE textbook author for ceramic material science.
industrialmaterial-scienceacademicfoundational
W.G. Lawrence
author, ceramic engineer
American - 20th Century
Brought industrial ceramic engineering methodology into studio practice.
technicalindustrialfoundational
Warren MacKenzie
studio potter, educator
American - 20th Century
University of Minnesota
Contributed substantially to understanding wood and reduction glaze behavior through decades of systematic practice and documentation.
reductionwood-firestudio

ceramic engineer, researcher, educator

Alfred University
